mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for developers and edge computing. It simplifies Kubernetes installation and management, making it ideal for local development and CI/CD environments. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to full Kubernetes distributions and reliance on the Snap package manager.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> This installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.

steps.title: Verify MicroK8s Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MicroK8s is running correctly and lists the nodes in the cluster.

```bash

microk8s status --wait-ready

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Waits until MicroK8s is fully initialized.
> Verifies that the node is ready.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI access.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This command will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to clean up permissions.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and resources. This step upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stops MicroK8s to prepare for the upgrade.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Starts MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verifies that the nodes are ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s is not installed or the Snap package is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install MicroK8s using 'sudo snap install microk8s --classic'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8080: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s is not running or has not been initialized.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s status --wait-ready' to ensure it is ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not linked to MicroK8s.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead or link it with 'sudo microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to enable addon: dns'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The DNS add-on may not be compatible with the current version of MicroK8s.

**Fix:**
Check the status of MicroK8s and ensure it is running, then try 'microk8s enable dns' again.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, improved add-on management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from 1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'microk8s kubectl get nodes' showing your node in a 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has Snap installed.
